canto_title: St. Bernard's Vision of the Celestial Rose and the Identification of the Blessed

scenes.summary: St. Bernard begins his teaching by describing the placement of the biblical matriarchs, including Rachel and Beatrice, within the celestial Rose.

scenes.scene_name: The Lineage of the Hebrew Mothers
scenes.summary: Bernard lists other prominent women of the Old Covenant and explains how they are layered within the petals of the Rose.

scenes.scene_name: The Structure of the Two Hemispheres
scenes.summary: The text describes the division of the Rose into two sides based on the arrival of Christ and the acceptance of His coming.

scenes.scene_name: The Hierarchy of the Great Doctors
scenes.summary: Bernard describes the vertical hierarchy of the great Christian teachers, such as John the Evangelist, Augustine, and Jerome.
The speaker explains that the placement of souls in the Rose is determined by God's grace rather than individual merit.
Bernard notices Dante's confusion and promises to untie the knots of his complex thoughts.

scenes.scene_name: The Nature of Eternal Joy and Grace
scenes.summary: The text describes how the King of Heaven distributes grace and joy to the spirits within the kingdom.
Bernard explains that the varying levels of the Rose are due to differences in the primordial intellect/capacity of the souls.
The lecture details the transition from the era of Jewish circumcision to the era of Christian grace and baptism.

scenes.scene_name: The Command to Focus on Christ
scenes.summary: Bernard instructs Dante to focus his gaze on the face of the Virgin to better understand Christ.
Dante witnesses a magnificent burst of joy and hears the angelic choir singing the 'Ave Maria'.
The entire blessed court responds to the angelic song, bringing serenity to Dante's vision.
An anonymous voice asks Bernard about the identity of the radiant angel gazing at the Virgin.
Bernard identifies the angel as the one who bore the palm of victory to Mary during the Incarnation.
Bernard directs Dante's eyes to the two most prominent figures who serve as the roots of the celestial Rose.
Bernard identifies the father of Adam and the Pope, the holder of the keys, in their respective positions.
The tour continues with the identification of Moses and Joshua within the heavenly hierarchy.
Bernard points out Anna, Rachel, and Lucia, and their positions opposite the great patriarchs.
Bernard urges Dante to focus on the first love, Christ, before time runs out.
Bernard concludes by advising Dante to seek grace from the Virgin so that he may follow the teaching with devotion.
